steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined sentence opens with “However” and reports that new studies show horizontal gene transfer is more common in eukaryotes than originally thought. Its role is determined by how it relates to the prior claim that HGT “has rarely been observed among eukaryotes.” The function question asks what this sentence does in context, not what the passage as a whole is about.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It implies that a common perception of horizontal gene transfer may be inaccurate.’: The prior sentence stated HGT is rarely seen in eukaryotes; the underlined sentence directly challenges that claim with new evidence, implying the common perception is wrong. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘It compares the frequencies with which horizontal gene transfer has been detected in two categories of organisms.’: The sentence says HGT is more common than thought in eukaryotes but does not compare eukaryote frequency to prokaryote frequency. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It argues that a particular direction of research concerning horizontal gene transfer is likely to be fruitless.’: The sentence presents a positive finding; it says nothing about research being unproductive. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It indicates a distinction between horizontal gene transfer and vertical gene transfer.’: That distinction is made in the first sentence, not in the underlined sentence. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
